I need to create a diverse dataset with 600 samples, where the 'outcome' column is the target variable. Can you design a preset that uses SMOTE or ADASYN to oversample minority classes and achieve a balanced distribution?<end_of_turn><eos>
<start_of_turn>model
<tool_call>
{"name": "BalancedDataGeneratorPreset", "arguments": {"n_samples": 600, "target_col": "outcome"}}
</tool_call><end_of_turn><eos>

To evaluate my model's ability to handle unexpected changes in data distribution, I need a dataset with 600 samples where 'feature5' is the target variable. The twist is that I want the relationship between the features and the target to shift abruptly, simulating real-world concept drift. This will help me understand how robust my model is when faced with sudden alterations in the underlying patterns of the data.<end_of_turn><eos>
<start_of_turn>model
<tool_call>
{"name": "ConceptDriftPreset", "arguments": {"n_samples": 600, "target_col": "feature5"}}
</tool_call><end_of_turn><eos>

I want to generate a collection of 200 time-series data points that capture daily patterns, using the 'datetime' column as the reference for time. The 'temperature' column should exhibit strong seasonal fluctuations with a repeating pattern every 24 hours and an amplitude of 0.8, simulating real-world temperature variations over time.<end_of_turn><eos>
<start_of_turn>model
<tool_call>
{"name": "SeasonalTimeSeriesPreset", "arguments": {"n_samples": 200, "time_col": "datetime", "seasonal_cols": ["temperature"], "period": 24, "amplitude": 0.8}}
</tool_call><end_of_turn><eos>

I'm looking to create a synthetic dataset with 1200 samples, where the target variable is 'group', and it has a specific imbalanced distribution. Can you use resampling or generative methods to create this dataset, ensuring that the target column has a custom distribution? I need a diverse dataset for tasks like drift detection and bias analysis.<end_of_turn><eos>
<start_of_turn>model
<tool_call>
{"name": "ImbalancedGeneratorPreset", "arguments": {"n_samples": 1200, "target_col": "group", "imbalance_ratio": 0.1}}
</tool_call><end_of_turn><eos>

Generate a time-series dataset of 800 samples where 'time' is the column representing time, and inject seasonal patterns into 'price' and 'volume'. The pattern should repeat every 3 periods. Please use default settings for the amplitude of the seasonal effect. I want to leverage both RealGenerator for learning base temporal dynamics and ScenarioInjector for adding a strong sinusoidal drift to create diverse data.<end_of_turn><eos>
<start_of_turn>model
<tool_call>
{"name": "SeasonalTimeSeriesPreset", "arguments": {"n_samples": 800, "time_col": "time", "seasonal_cols": ["price", "volume"], "period": 3, "amplitude": 1.0}}
